text:
Early incoming transfer class rankings at https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ:

1. Louisville
2. Texas
3. Xavier
4. Texas A&M
5. Providence
6. Iowa State
7. Houston
8. Syracuse
9. Tennessee
10. Ole Miss

Last season, Michigan had the #1 incoming class and was #1 in overall transfer activity. https://t.co/bAtxMPh4ej

Two massive signings for Louisville. 

Bidunga is one of the best transfer portal prospects we've ever seen. He should be awesome for Pat Kelsey. https://t.co/oUYCPpfsE4

Garwey Dual has a better projected rating than any player Syracuse had last season, so this is a great pickup for Cuse. 

Dual is in the top 5 projected defenders in the portal among guards. Really good steal rate, and had a big impact on McNeese defensively while on the floor. https://t.co/feO9aaLroL

Love this pickup for Texas. 

Isaiah Johnson is the 2nd highest rated offensive player in the portal at https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ. Great scorer, great playmaker, good shooting efficiency in all three areas. https://t.co/WVxOirlGtK https://t.co/EbzshOTXuc

The EvanMiya Merch store:

https://t.co/GIFwwKar5I https://t.co/s0MnFIRfYd
Love seeing @DavidWCobb rock the EvanMiya hat live on the @EyeOnCBBPodcast! https://t.co/DrnSawqW5X
Here is Yaxel Lendeborg's profile from last offseason: https://t.co/8LjRlnkPVL

As a follow-up on Allen Graves, it's pretty rare to find a guy who is so good in so many different skill areas, without having many weaknesses. 

Similar in that way to Yaxel Lendeborg. https://t.co/xt6qRyuwyD https://t.co/Tg5zDwEDPy

Allen Graves will become the #1 transfer at https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ, moving ahead of Flory Bidunga.

Quite simply a tremendous player with a ton of potential to pop even more in his sophomore year. Does pretty much everything at a really high level. https://t.co/jTJYMsWDKx https://t.co/c5ayitrIE3

One thing I will point out as people look for metrics that predict transfer success: 

On/off splits are less predictive of future success when players transfer. My research has shown that there is much less correlation between On/off splits and performance when a player changes https://t.co/VJV4kcXy8f

Teams with 10+ players entered into the transfer portal, according to https://t.co/cegyfz96ax:

UT Martin
N. Illinois
Canisius
Boston College
W. Michigan
Weber St.
Washington St.
Wagner
UTEP
UNCG
Saint Francis
Le Moyne
Jacksonville
Fresno State https://t.co/Yey2gcLUbv

You can make filtered shortlists like this if you have access to the Front Office Suite tool at https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ. It also includes estimated NIL values, which I have hidden in the graphic above.

Looking for a sharpshooter transfer? There are 30 players in the portal who are 97th percentile or better in their 3PT% projection at https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ.

They are all ordered here by their overall transfer rating, with Rob Wright and John Blackwell leading the pack 👇 https://t.co/UkxQYYqdaR

Last year's portal cycle had 49 five-star transfers based on the rating system at https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ.

We are already at 41 this offseason. The portal has been open less than 48 hours.

Newest ones towards the top:
Robert Wright
Aidan Sherrell
Larry Johnson
PJ Haggerty

On the first possession of the title game, Michigan threw a curveball at UConn.

On today's show:
🎙️ @EricFawcett_ joins to break down the Final Four
↔️ Roster + portal trends we expect to see

Audio: https://t.co/6iiwvwQT56
Video: https://t.co/EBgfnqmwDZ
https://t.co/EBgfnqmwDZ

Teams have to be efficient with the money they spend, while reacting to current market trends. 

That's what the Front Office Suite product at https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ does, among other things. It's been very cool to see how quickly it's being adopted. https://t.co/vVjNpLbrq7
You can see full bracket simulation results here: https://t.co/ZiIyQcxhZx

To track how good the https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ bracket simulations were, I had a bunch of people add my sims to a massive contest to see the results. 

The median bracket sim was in the 64th percentile (anything above 50 is the goal). 

The best sim was in the top 0.1% at ESPN: https://t.co/6I0gZ248I4

My bracket ended up finishing in the 99th percentile, and I won every bracket group I was in.

It always takes a stroke of luck for things to break your way, but this does now make it 99th percentile brackets for me in 2 of the past 3 years. https://t.co/TB370YcgKF https://t.co/ZtUVvMfkuC

Tarris Reed is almost smiling up at the podium in the postgame press-conference. 

He is disappointed obviously, but he mentioned that he's happy for his former Michigan teammates. https://t.co/EIgx9zTU27

Michigan had the least roster retention of any team in the modern portal era: Just 34% of their minutes were played by returning players. The previous 4 champs were all above 50%.

It was not a given that Dusty May's group of transfers would work. All credit to him and the staff.

Despite being much maligned by North Carolina fans and many others, Cadeau had the profile of a point guard last offseason who could be a major difference-maker on a different team. He was a top 15 transfer at https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ. 

Cadeau stock could not be higher now. https://t.co/geNHuWS11v

We had more talent this year in college basketball arguably than any year in history.

Michigan was the clear best team this year, beating every team in the tournament comfortably.

In light of that context, they have to be in the conversation as one of the greatest ever.

Yaxel Lendeborg played 36 minutes tonight on a bad knee. 

He was clearly hobbled, but he showed tremendous toughness. He finished with 13 points, 9 of them coming in the 2nd half. 

One of the gutsiest performances you will see. What a player.

Final: Michigan 69, UConn 63

Michigan caps off one of the greatest tournament runs with a win over the best program in our modern era. 

They never really looked like losing tonight, despite UConn's best efforts.

MASSIVE sequence for Michigan. A block on one end to prevent a UConn fastbreak bucket, and then a great play by Gayle leads to a wide open McKenney three. 

UConn is very close to losing its first title game in program history.
This might be the worst three-point shooting title game we've had in a while. Nothing is going in!
It's been so mystifying to see all these quality looks not fall for UConn. https://t.co/izWvClaBSf
Roddy Gayle has been a real difference-maker tonight. Only 4 points, but he's been in the middle of Michigan's positive plays all night.

UConn has been offensive rebounding at an extraordinary rate tonight: 45%.

17 offensive rebounds against Michigan's tremendous frontcourt.
Karaban and Mullins are 3-13 on triples today.

UConn needs a patented Kill Shot in the worst way right now, something they have struggled to do most of the season. 

Michigan has only allowed three 10-0 runs this year.

UConn has 14 total fouls. Michigan has 5. 

HUGE disparity there.

UConn's gameplan is perfect. Michigan has:

- Zero threes
- Zero fastbreak points
- Zero points off turnovers
- On pace for under 60 possessions

Michigan still leads by 4 at half.

Michigan's last two losses were slowwww games: 63 possessions each. 

We are on pace for 59 possessions tonight. UConn is doing everything they can to drag possessions out as long as possible.
Dusty May frantically tried to call a timeout before UConn's last possession but none of the refs were paying attention.

A quick 6 points in 49 seconds and the Michigan crowd is LOUD. 

Yaxel Lendeborg STILL hasn't gone to the bench yet 17 minutes in.

Michigan has zero points off turnovers. 

They had 26 against Arizona on Saturday.
Yaxel doesn't even look comfortable going into a normal layup motion, he's just hoping to get tackled to the ground on the way to the basket.

Yaxel, dealing with a major injury, is the only Michigan player who hasn't sat yet.

Very puzzling considering he's not moving like normal.

Like Saturday, UConn has the edge in three-point shooting early. 

They are 3/8, compared to Michigan's 0/5.

Malachi Smith, have a day!

With Tarris Reed and Silas Demary on the bench, the Dayton transfer has been massive in the last several minutes. It's the little contributions that can make a big difference.

Morez Johnson is a clear part of the Michigan gameplan offensively, he is eating up the Huskies on the interior early. 

Mara has been playing more outside early.

Michigan and UConn square off in the national title game in a few hours!

Dan Hurley is inevitable, but faces the toughest opponent of his career in Dusty May's monstrous Wolverines. 

Prediction: Michigan 78, UConn 70 https://t.co/bHdLGYz4ev
My least favorite thing about the portal is when good players decides to transfer and their own team's "fans" start trashing their game online.

Top 5 transfers available at https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ, and where they would have ranked in last year's cycle:

1. Flory Bidunga, Kansas (#2 last year)
2. Acaden Lewis, Villanova (#4)
3. Sananda Fru, Louisville (#5)
4. Isaiah Johnson, Colorado (#6)
5. John Blackwell, Wisconsin (#9)
Dusty May told Cadeau to fire up passes off the backboard 🤯 https://t.co/ExP2RhdDCu
It’s a shame Arizona’s season ended in the manner it did. This was a really talented team that probably would have been a national title favorite in almost any other season.

For all the marbles on Monday. 

Michigan is a wagon. Dan Hurley never loses this deep in the tournament. Something has to give. https://t.co/Hxa1DCxB6p

Michigan has been the best team for the majority of the season, though they only spent a single week atop the AP poll.

Michigan was ranked #1 for 116 days this year at https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ. Arizona was never once the top-ranked team.

"You can take Monday night off Doug!"

Some Arizona fans just shouted at Doug Sirmons. He acknowledged them with a smirk and a slight nod of the head.

Aday Mara has more points than both of Arizona's starting frontcourt players. He's been unstoppable. 

26 points, 11-15 FG, 7 rebounds, 3 blocks, 3 assists. 

Just as huge a transfer for Michigan as Yaxel was.

If Michigan wins the national title, it has an argument to be the greatest team of all time. 

In a year with more talent in college basketball than ever before, they are blitzing everybody with a pulse.
Head coaches who have been to the Final Four before are crushing the coaches who have not.

Arizona literally is never rattled, but they are shell-shocked right now. 

Michigan is obliterating them. I can't believe what I'm seeing.

For the first time this season, Arizona will be forced to play a modern offense.

Gotta take more threes if you want to have a chance.

This was insane. UConn missed a few as well, but Illinois had so many shots that rimmed in and out. 

Really tough way to go out. https://t.co/MwU3es8N1P
Arizona guards: 8 points, 2-8 FG, 4 turnovers

Elliot Cadeau has missed 12 shots.

Michigan leads by 16.

Michigan has scored 48 first-half points, the most allowed by Arizona in any game this season.

The previous high was 46 against Florida in the first game of the season.

Michigan is making Arizona look mortal for the first time this season. 

They have no answers for Aday Mara off the pick-and-roll.

You have to wonder if Arizona will need to shoot more threes in order to catch up to Michigan. 

Just 5 threes taken for Arizona compared to 12 for Michigan.

In this first half, Michigan has had:

8-0 run
6-0 run
7-0 run
7-0 run (ongoing)

They just keep hammering Arizona in droves.

Jaden Bradley picks up his third foul on an offensive push-off after not getting a call his direction a second before. 

Brutal blow for Arizona.
RODDY GAYLE!
I don't think Yaxel is slapping the floor in frustration unless he's worried he may not be able to keep playing. https://t.co/OE3aqaFkqe

Elliot Cadeau averages 8 shots per game. He's already hit that in the first 9 minutes. 

He's only made two, but he's been excellent in every other area: 4 assists, 2 rebounds, 2 steals. His energy has been off the charts early.

Elliot Cadeau has Arizona's ball handlers in hell. 

Two steals leading to breakaway scores, forcing an Arizona timeout.

Michigan is FLYING out of the gates. 

Already up 10-1 with 4 different Wolverines on the board.

🚨 Yaxel Lendeborg has two fouls in the first 1:22. 

Horrible development for Michigan.

The crazy thing about this Michigan Arizona game:

Michigan has allowed just three 10-0 runs against them all season. Arizona has only allowed two. 

If either team gets a big run tonight, it will feel massive.

Tarris Reed in the NCAA tournament: 20.8 points and 13.0 rebounds per game. 

As dominant a tourney run as you will ever find.
Illinois only had 3 assist tonight, the fewest in the last 5 seasons.
UConn survived Illinois's late push. https://t.co/Vni3lI9Y1K
Illinois finishes the season 4-8 against top 20 opponents at https://t.co/cegyfz8ykZ.
Well crap. The magical week 6 AP poll stat will be true again this year. https://t.co/WnNDVQ2rVX

Final: UConn 71, Illinois 62

UConn brought it tonight in a way Illinois did not. From my seat a few feet from the court, it just seemed like Illinois didn't have the energy level required to win a national title. 

The Huskies weren't perfect, but they did enough to advance.
Both teams are shooting 36% from the field... yuck.
It hasn't been there for Keaton Wagler from outside, but he's been excellent at the rim: 5-6 from two tonight.

Illinois KILL SHOT!

It's the Illini's 39th 10-0 run of the year, and just the 10th conceded by UConn all season.
In a different game, Illinois has 4 extra buckets because these 50-50 attempts at the rim go down. https://t.co/IJouzY7z4p

Illinois has TWO assists in this game.

The fewest they've had in a full game in the last five seasons is 5 assists.
After 14 first-half threes from Illinois, they have only attempted 4 in the 2nd half so far.
Dan Hurley just lost his mind at one ref, then sauntered over to a different ref to appeal.
Meanwhile on the other end, Solo Ball's three bounces twice on the back rim before dropping in. https://t.co/IJouzY7z4p

Illinois has had 3 or 4 shots at the rim that looked like they were going in and then rimmed out. 

Really brutal misfortune there, and it's costing them.

Very hit-or-miss game from David Mirkovic tonight. 

He just hit a big three and has several blocks, but he just lost the ball twice in the last couple minutes, and he's got three fouls.

It is very clear that Keaton Wagler is the only Illinois player who can create their own offense tonight. 

He needs to keep forcing the issue because he's not getting much help.

29 first-half points for Illinois is the 3rd fewest they've had this year, but they've been a 2nd half team all tournament.

The two games with lower first-half totals have been the past 2 games:
24 vs Houston
28 vs Iowa

A majority of the first-half shots in this UConn/Illinois game have been threes (50.7%).

In last year's final four, that number was 42% for Florida/Auburn and 34% for Duke/Houston.

An absurd three-point shooting contest in this game, which UConn is comfortably winning.

Half: UConn 37, Illinois 29

The difference in this game is three-point shooting. UConn is 7-18 (39%), Illinois is 3-14 (21%).

Outside of Tarris Reed, neither team has found a consistent inside threat.
Half of Illinois's shots are barely grazing the rim. It's pretty appalling.
Illinois is in the bonus with 9:21 remaining in the first half.
Zero assists because none of their threes have gone in yet. That can't continue. https://t.co/Tjz6QO1cPZ

Tarris Reed is continuing his historically dominant tournament tonight, he has 7 points and 4 rebounds in the first 7 minutes.

Combine that with UConn's three-point shooting coming back early (3-7 so far), and they are almost impossible to beat. ILL has to find an answer.

Every time Braylon Mullins shoots a three now I hear Ian Eagle saying "Mullins tosses it up!"

Big three to open the scoring for UConn.

The Illinois contingent in Lucas Oil Stadium is LOUD. Feels twice as vocal as the UConn fanbase. 

And there is a TON of orange.
So grateful to be here for this momentous occasion. Doesn’t get better than Final Four Saturday! https://t.co/cSz0EC7mjc

Arizona and Michigan square off in arguably the most anticipated game in years.

There's really not much to separate these two teams. Yaxel Lendeborg is the best player on the floor, but Zona's elite consistency is what makes them hard to beat.

Prediction: Michigan 82, Zona 79 https://t.co/fAo9SWu5Mp

The Final Four tips off with Illinois vs UConn in a game I think is genuinely a coin-flip. 

Illinois has the slightly better roster, and Wagler has been outstanding. UConn has been playing their best basketball in huge games though.

Prediction: 72-72, headed to overtime. https://t.co/0URfjao8c7
